title: NEO 4.0 Common Provenance/Timestamp Envelope
_status: INSTALLED 4.0-P3-A (from the KEPT 4.0 Session-0 draft). Live spine schema. Reconciles with (does not fork) neo:artifact_provenance and neo:checkpoint.
description: Every 4.0 hybrid structured-evidence file EMBEDS this envelope under key `_provenance`. It is the single carrier of identity, version, custody, hashing, scope, and input lineage. Fail-closed (A7): if any REQUIRED field below is missing, unknown, or unresolvable, the consuming gate/auditor MUST BLOCK and require classification/authority before proceeding — never proceed on a partial envelope.

artifact_id.description: Stable id of this evidence artifact.

artifact_class.description: REUSES the live neo:artifact_classes vocabulary. Judging classes carry stricter edit gates (D6).
artifact_class._reconcile: First 5 are verbatim from .neo/schema/artifact_classes.json. The last 4 are 4.0 additive classes proposed for the routing layer; see D6/D10 reconciliation. UNKNOWN class => BLOCKED (A7).

schema_id.description: id of the schema this artifact claims to satisfy. REQUIRED (A7): missing => BLOCKED.

schema_version.description: Version string of that schema. REQUIRED (A7): unknown/missing => BLOCKED.

role.description: NEO role that authored (e.g. builder, master-orchestrator, constraint-editor).

model_class.description: Capability CLASS (Q2 — classes bound now, exact model NAMES live in the routing-log/policy layer, not here).

model_name.description: Optional concrete model name, resolved from the routing layer; nullable so the schema does not hard-code names (Q2).

not_applicable_reason.description: Only for artifacts that legitimately have no validator yet (e.g. a raw builder proof pre-audit). A null identity WITHOUT this reason => BLOCKED (A7).

validator_identity.description: REQUIRED (A7). validator model_class MUST be >= producer model_class per D6 (validator >= producer).

created_at.description: ISO-8601, CALLER-SUPPLIED. Mirrors neo:checkpoint convention: agents do not self-generate time.

content_hash.description: Self-hash of the artifact body (envelope excluded from its own hash input). Used by rollover stale/hash checks (A6/D7) and audit-bundle integrity (D3).

declared_paths.description: Root-relative './...' globs this artifact's work was confined to. Reconciles with session_contract.approved_paths (but root-relative, never absolute).

source_input_packets.description: Lineage (A3): every input packet this artifact consumed, by id + content_hash. Empty array allowed only for a root spec. Reproducibility + custody.

gate_ref.description: Decision-log id binding to ./.neo/gates/HUMAN_GATE_LEDGER.json. SAME convention as neo:artifact_provenance.gate_ref and neo:unlock_record.gate_ref. Null only for un-gated low-risk batched artifacts (D5); judging-class artifacts REQUIRE non-null (fail-closed).
